question: What are the famous title of the artists associated with volumes with more than 2 weeks on top?
context: CREATE TABLE artist (Famous_Title VARCHAR, Artist_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE volume (Artist_ID VARCHAR, Weeks_on_Top INTEGER)
original_sql: SELECT T1.Famous_Title FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > 2
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_artist.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    Famous_Title AS famous_title,
    Artist_ID AS artist_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'artist') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_volume.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    Artist_ID AS artist_id,
    Weeks_on_Top AS weeks_on_top
FROM {{ source('raw', 'volume') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: artist
      columns:
        - name: artist_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: volume
      columns:
        - name: artist_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_artist
    columns:
      - name: famous_title
      - name: artist_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_volume
    columns:
      - name: artist_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: weeks_on_top
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_famous_title_artists.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH artist AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_artist') }}

),

volume AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_volume') }}

)

SELECT artist.famous_title
FROM artist
JOIN volume ON artist.artist_id = artist.artist_id
WHERE volume.weeks_on_top > 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_famous_title_artists
    description: "What are the famous title of the artists associated with volumes with more than 2 weeks on top."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per artist-volume pair"
    columns:
      - name: famous_title
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_hardware_model_name.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_screen_mode') }}

),

phone AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_phone') }}

SELECT DISTINCT phone.hardware_model_name

JOIN phone ON screen_mode.graphics_mode = phone.screen_mode
WHERE screen_mode.type = "Graphics" OR t2.company_name = "Nokia Corporation"

question: find the program owners that have some programs in both morning and night time.
context: CREATE TABLE broadcast (program_id VARCHAR, Time_of_day VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE program (owner VARCHAR, program_id VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT t1.owner FROM program AS t1 JOIN broadcast AS t2 ON t1.program_id = t2.program_id WHERE t2.Time_of_day = "Morning" INTERSECT SELECT t1.owner FROM program AS t1 JOIN broadcast AS t2 ON t1.program_id = t2.program_id WHERE t2.Time_of_day = "Night"
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_broadcast.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    program_id,
    Time_of_day AS time_of_day
FROM {{ source('raw', 'broadcast') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_program.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    owner,
    program_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'program') }}
</file>

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: broadcast
      columns:
        - name: program_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: program
      columns:
        - name: program_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_broadcast
    columns:
      - name: program_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: time_of_day
  - name: stg_program
    columns:
      - name: owner
      - name: program_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_program_owners_some.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH program AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_program') }}

),

broadcast AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_broadcast') }}

)

SELECT program.owner
FROM program
JOIN broadcast ON broadcast.program_id = broadcast.program_id
WHERE broadcast.time_of_day = "Morning"
INTERSECT
SELECT program.owner
FROM program
JOIN broadcast ON broadcast.program_id = broadcast.program_id
WHERE broadcast.time_of_day = "Night"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_program_owners_some
    description: "Find the program owners that have some programs in both morning and night time.."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per distinct result in set operation"
    columns:
      - name: owner
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

question: What is the code of airport that has fewest number of flights?
context: CREATE TABLE FLIGHTS (DestAirport VARCHAR, SourceAirport VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE AIRPORTS (AirportCode VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT T1.AirportCode FROM AIRPORTS AS T1 JOIN FLIGHTS AS T2 ON T1.AirportCode = T2.DestAirport OR T1.AirportCode = T2.SourceAirport GROUP BY T1.AirportCode ORDER BY COUNT(*) LIMIT 1
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_flights.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    DestAirport AS dest_airport,
    SourceAirport AS source_airport
FROM {{ source('raw', 'FLIGHTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_airports.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    AirportCode AS airport_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'AIRPORTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: FLIGHTS
      columns:
        - name: dest_airport
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: AIRPORTS
      columns:
        - name: airport_code
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_flights
    columns:
      - name: dest_airport
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: source_airport
  - name: stg_airports
    columns:
      - name: airport_code
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_code_airport_fewest.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH airports AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }}

),

flights AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}

)

SELECT airports.airport_code
FROM airports
JOIN flights ON airports.airport_code = flights.dest_airport OR airports.airport_code = flights.source_airport
GROUP BY airports.airport_code
ORDER BY COUNT(*)
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

  - name: rpt_code_airport_fewest
    description: "What is the code of airport that has fewest number of flights."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per airport_code"
    columns:
      - name: airport_code
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

original_sql: SELECT t3.fname, t3.lname FROM club AS t1 JOIN member_of_club AS t2 ON t1.clubid = t2.clubid JOIN student AS t3 ON t2.stuid = t3.stuid WHERE t1.clubname = "Hopkins Student Enterprises" AND t3.sex = "M"

      - name: clubname
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: fname
      - name: lname
      - name: stuid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: sex
  - name: stg_member_of_club
    columns:
      - name: clubid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: stuid

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_male_members_club.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH club AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_club') }}

),

member_of_club AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_member_of_club') }}

),

student AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}

SELECT student.fname, student.lname
FROM club

original_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year < 2009 INTERSECT SELECT t1.name FROM visitor AS t1 JOIN visit AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.visitor_id JOIN museum AS t3 ON t3.Museum_ID = t2.Museum_ID WHERE t3.open_year > 2011

SELECT
    Museum_ID AS museum_id,
    open_year

      - name: visit

          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

    columns:

      - name: id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_museum
    columns:
      - name: museum_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: open_year
  - name: stg_visit
    columns:
      - name: visitor_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: museum_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_visitor_visited.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH visitor AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_visitor') }}

),

visit AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_visit') }}

),

museum AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_museum') }}

)

SELECT visitor.name
FROM visitor
JOIN visit ON visitor.id = visit.visitor_id
JOIN museum ON museum.museum_id = museum.museum_id
WHERE museum.open_year < 2009
INTERSECT
SELECT visitor.name
FROM visitor
JOIN visit ON visitor.id = visit.visitor_id
JOIN museum ON museum.museum_id = museum.museum_id
WHERE museum.open_year > 2011
</file>

    columns:
      - name: name

          - not_null

original_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM courses AS T1 JOIN student_course_attendance AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T2.student_id = 171
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_courses.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    course_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'courses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_course_attendance.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    course_id,
    student_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'student_course_attendance') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: courses
      columns:
        - name: course_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: student_course_attendance
      columns:
        - name: course_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_courses
    columns:
      - name: course_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_student_course_attendance
    columns:
      - name: course_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: student_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_courses_counts.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH courses AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_courses') }}

),

student_course_attendance AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student_course_attendance') }}

)

SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM courses
JOIN student_course_attendance ON courses.course_id = courses.course_id
WHERE student_course_attendance.student_id = 171
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_courses_counts
    description: "How many courses do the student whose id is 171 attend."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per courses-student_course_attendance pair"
    columns:
      - name: *
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_products_extremes.sql">

question: In 2014, what are the id and rank of the team that has the largest average number of attendance?
context: CREATE TABLE team (team_id VARCHAR, rank VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE home_game (team_id VARCHAR, year VARCHAR, attendance INTEGER)
original_sql: SELECT T2.team_id, T2.rank FROM home_game AS T1 JOIN team AS T2 ON T1.team_id = T2.team_id WHERE T1.year = 2014 GROUP BY T1.team_id ORDER BY AVG(T1.attendance) DESC LIMIT 1
dbt_dag:

{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    team_id,
    rank
FROM {{ source('raw', 'team') }}
</file>

{% config(materialized='view') %}

    team_id,
    year,
    attendance

            - not_null

      - name: home_game
      columns:
        - name: team_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_team
    columns:
      - name: team_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: rank
  - name: stg_home_game
    columns:
      - name: team_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: year
      - name: attendance
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_2014_rank_team.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH home_game AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_home_game') }}

),

team AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_team') }}

)

SELECT team.team_id, team.rank
FROM home_game
JOIN team ON team.team_id = team.team_id
WHERE home_game.year = 2014
GROUP BY team.team_id
ORDER BY AVG(home_game.attendance) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

{% config(materialized='table') %}

stadium AS (

question: report the total number of degrees granted between 1998 and 2002.

dbt_dag:

    id

          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_campuses
    columns:
      - name: campus
      - name: id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_degrees

      - name: degrees
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique

WITH campuses AS (

),

degrees AS (

    color_code

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_product_color.sql">

    columns:

          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_first_last_name.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH student AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}

),

participates_in AS (

    SELECT *

          - not_null

        tests:

original_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products AS t1 JOIN product_characteristics AS t2 ON t1.product_id = t2.product_id JOIN CHARACTERISTICS AS t3 ON t2.characteristic_id = t3.characteristic_id WHERE t1.product_name = "laurel"
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_characteristics.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    characteristic_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'CHARACTERISTICS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    product_id,
    product_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_product_characteristics.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    product_id,
    characteristic_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'product_characteristics') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: CHARACTERISTICS
      columns:
        - name: characteristic_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: products
      columns:
        - name: product_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: product_characteristics
      columns:
        - name: product_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_characteristics
    columns:
      - name: characteristic_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: product_name
  - name: stg_product_characteristics
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: characteristic_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_products_counts.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH products AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }}

),

product_characteristics AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_product_characteristics') }}

),

characteristics AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_characteristics') }}

)

SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM products
JOIN product_characteristics ON products.product_id = products.product_id
JOIN characteristics ON characteristics.characteristic_id = characteristics.characteristic_id
WHERE products.product_name = "laurel"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_products_counts
    description: "How many characteristics does the product named "laurel" have."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per products-product_characteristics pair"
    columns:
      - name: *
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_owner_paid_treatments.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH owners AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_owners') }}

),

dogs AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_dogs') }}

),

treatments AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_treatments') }}

)

FROM owners
JOIN dogs ON owners.owner_id = owners.owner_id
JOIN treatments ON dogs.dog_id = dogs.dog_id

ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

        tests:

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_names_clubs_member.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH club AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_club') }}

),

member_of_club AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_member_of_club') }}

),

student AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}

)

SELECT DISTINCT club.clubname
FROM club
JOIN member_of_club ON member_of_club.clubid = member_of_club.clubid
JOIN student ON member_of_club.stuid = member_of_club.stuid

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_names_enzymes_medicine.sql">

WITH enzyme AS (

{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH appellations AS (

    SELECT *

),

wine AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_wine') }}

)

SELECT appellations.county
FROM appellations
JOIN wine ON wine.appelation = wine.appelation

original_sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.max_speed FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.storm_id = T2.storm_id GROUP BY T1.storm_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_storm.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    name,
    max_speed,
    storm_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'storm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_affected_region.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    storm_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'affected_region') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: storm
      columns:
        - name: storm_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: affected_region
      columns:
        - name: storm_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_storm
    columns:
      - name: name
      - name: max_speed
      - name: storm_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_affected_region
    columns:
      - name: storm_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_storm_name_max.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH storm AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_storm') }}

),

affected_region AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_affected_region') }}

)

SELECT storm.name, storm.max_speed
FROM storm
JOIN affected_region ON storm.storm_id = storm.storm_id
GROUP BY storm.storm_id
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_storm_name_max
    description: "What is the storm name and max speed which affected the greatest number of regions."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per storm_id"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: max_speed
</file>

original_sql: SELECT document_id FROM Documents WHERE document_status_code = "done" AND document_type_code = "Paper" EXCEPT SELECT document_id FROM Documents JOIN Ref_Shipping_Agents ON Documents.shipping_agent_code = Ref_Shipping_Agents.shipping_agent_code WHERE Ref_Shipping_Agents.shipping_agent_name = "USPS"

  - name: stg_documents

      - name: document_type_code

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_document_ids_documents.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH documents AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }}

),

ref_shipping_agents AS (

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_physicians_affiliated.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH physician AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_physician') }}

),

affiliated_with AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_affiliated_with') }}

),

department AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }}

)

SELECT department.name

JOIN department ON affiliated_with.department = department.department_id
WHERE department.name = 'Psychiatry'

    columns:
      - name: name
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_department_largest.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH professor AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_professor') }}

),

department AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }}

)

SELECT department.dept_name, professor.dept_code
FROM professor
JOIN department ON professor.dept_code = professor.dept_code
WHERE professor.prof_high_degree = 'ph.d.'
GROUP BY professor.dept_code
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

    columns:

        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: dept_code
</file>

original_sql: SELECT T3.name FROM assignedto AS T1 JOIN projects AS T2 ON T1.project = T2.code JOIN scientists AS T3 ON T1.scientist = T3.SSN WHERE T2.name = 'Matter of Time' INTERSECT SELECT T3.name FROM assignedto AS T1 JOIN projects AS T2 ON T1.project = T2.code JOIN scientists AS T3 ON T1.scientist = T3.SSN WHERE T2.name = 'A Puzzling Parallax'

        tests:

          - unique
      - name: scientist
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_scientist_worked.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH assignedto AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_assignedto') }}

),

projects AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_projects') }}

),

scientists AS (

WITH train_station AS (

),

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_average_grade_point.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH enrolled_in AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_enrolled_in') }}

),

gradeconversion AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_gradeconversion') }}

),

student AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}

SELECT AVG(gradeconversion.gradepoint)
FROM enrolled_in

original_sql: SELECT T1.Code, T1.Fate, T2.Name FROM mission AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_mission.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    Code AS code,
    Fate AS fate,
    Ship_ID AS ship_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'mission') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ship.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    Name AS name,
    Ship_ID AS ship_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'ship') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: mission
      columns:
        - name: ship_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: ship
      columns:
        - name: ship_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_mission
    columns:
      - name: code
      - name: fate
      - name: ship_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_ship
    columns:
      - name: name
      - name: ship_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_codes_fates_missions.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH mission AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_mission') }}

),

ship AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_ship') }}

)

SELECT mission.code, mission.fate, ship.name
FROM mission
JOIN ship ON mission.ship_id = mission.ship_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_codes_fates_missions
    description: "Show codes and fates of missions, and names of ships involved.."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per mission-ship pair"
    columns:
      - name: code
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: fate
      - name: name
</file>

          - not_null

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_members_bootup_baltimore.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH club AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_club') }}

),

member_of_club AS (

    SELECT *

student AS (

    SELECT *

    columns:

          - not_null

original_sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.balance + T2.balance FROM accounts AS T1 JOIN checking AS T2 ON T1.custid = T2.custid JOIN savings AS T3 ON T1.custid = T3.custid WHERE T3.balance < T2.balance

          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: accounts
      columns:
        - name: custid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_checking
    columns:
      - name: balance
      - name: custid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_savings
    columns:
      - name: balance
      - name: custid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_accounts
    columns:
      - name: name
      - name: custid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_total_checking.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH accounts AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_accounts') }}

),

checking AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_checking') }}

),

savings AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_savings') }}

)

SELECT accounts.name, checking.balance + checking.balance
FROM accounts
JOIN checking ON checking.custid = checking.custid
JOIN savings ON checking.custid = checking.custid
WHERE checking.balance < checking.balance
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_name_total_checking
    description: "Find the name and total checking and savings balance of the accounts whose savings balance is lower than corresponding checking balance.."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per accounts-checking pair"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: balance
</file>

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}

    SELECT *

question: find the name of employee who was awarded the most times in the evaluation.
context: CREATE TABLE evaluation (Employee_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE employee (name VARCHAR, Employee_ID VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM employee AS t1 JOIN evaluation AS t2 ON t1.Employee_ID = t2.Employee_ID GROUP BY t2.Employee_ID ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_evaluation.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    Employee_ID AS employee_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'evaluation') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_employee.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    name,
    Employee_ID AS employee_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'employee') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: evaluation
      columns:
        - name: employee_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: employee
      columns:
        - name: employee_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_evaluation
    columns:
      - name: employee_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_employee
    columns:
      - name: name
      - name: employee_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_employee_was.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH employee AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }}

),

evaluation AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_evaluation') }}

)

SELECT employee.name
FROM employee
JOIN evaluation ON evaluation.employee_id = evaluation.employee_id
GROUP BY evaluation.employee_id
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_name_employee_was
    description: "Find the name of employee who was awarded the most times in the evaluation.."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per employee_id"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_assets_parts_fault.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH assets AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_assets') }}

),

asset_parts AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_asset_parts') }}

),

fault_log AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_fault_log') }}

)

SELECT assets.asset_id, assets.asset_details
FROM assets
JOIN asset_parts ON assets.asset_id = assets.asset_id
GROUP BY assets.asset_id
HAVING COUNT(*) = 2
INTERSECT
SELECT assets.asset_id, assets.asset_details
FROM assets
JOIN fault_log ON assets.asset_id = assets.asset_id
GROUP BY assets.asset_id
HAVING COUNT(*) < 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_assets_parts_fault
    description: "Which assets have 2 parts and have less than 2 fault logs? List the asset id and detail.."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per asset_id"
    columns:
      - name: asset_id
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: asset_details
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_distinct_positions_players.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH country AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_country') }}

),

match_season AS (

    SELECT *

)

SELECT DISTINCT match_season.position

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_institution_counts.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH institution AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_institution') }}

),

protein AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_protein') }}

),

building AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_building') }}

)

SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM institution
JOIN protein ON institution.institution_id = institution.institution_id
JOIN building ON institution.building_id = institution.building_id
WHERE building.floors >= 20
</file>

models:

    description: "How many proteins are associated with an institution in a building with at least 20 floors."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per institution-protein pair"
    columns:
      - name: *
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_nationality_swimmer.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH swimmer AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_swimmer') }}

),

record AS (

    SELECT *

)

question: What are all the policy types of the customer that has the most policies listed?
context: CREATE TABLE customers (customer_id VARCHAR, customer_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE available_policies (policy_type_code VARCHAR, policy_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customers_policies (customer_id VARCHAR, policy_id VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t3.policy_type_code FROM customers AS t1 JOIN customers_policies AS t2 ON t1.customer_id = t2.customer_id JOIN available_policies AS t3 ON t2.policy_id = t3.policy_id WHERE t1.customer_name = (SELECT t1.customer_name FROM customers AS t1 JOIN customers_policies AS t2 ON t1.customer_id = t2.customer_id GROUP BY t1.customer_name ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1)
dbt_dag:

{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    customer_id,
    customer_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}

<file path="models/staging/stg_available_policies.sql">

SELECT
    policy_type_code,
    policy_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'available_policies') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers_policies.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    customer_id,
    policy_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers_policies') }}
</file>

    tables:
      - name: customers

            - unique

      - name: available_policies
      columns:
        - name: policy_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: customers_policies
      columns:
        - name: customer_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: customer_name
  - name: stg_available_policies
    columns:
      - name: policy_type_code
      - name: policy_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_customers_policies
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: policy_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_policy_types_customer.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH customers AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}

),

customers_policies AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers_policies') }}

),

available_policies AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_available_policies') }}

)

SELECT DISTINCT available_policies.policy_type_code
FROM customers
JOIN customers_policies ON customers.customer_id = customers.customer_id
JOIN available_policies ON available_policies.policy_id = available_policies.policy_id
WHERE customers.customer_name = (
SELECT customers.customer_name
FROM customers
JOIN customers_policies ON customers.customer_id = customers.customer_id
GROUP BY customers.customer_name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_policy_types_customer
    description: "What are all the policy types of the customer that has the most policies listed."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per customer_name"
    columns:
      - name: policy_type_code
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_drivers_first_last.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH drivers AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }}

),

pitstops AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_pitstops') }}

),

results AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }}

)

SELECT drivers.forename, drivers.surname, drivers.driverid
FROM drivers
JOIN pitstops ON drivers.driverid = drivers.driverid
GROUP BY drivers.driverid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 8
UNION

FROM drivers
JOIN results ON drivers.driverid = drivers.driverid
GROUP BY drivers.driverid
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5

question: Find the name of dorms which have TV Lounge but no Study Room as amenity.
context: CREATE TABLE dorm (dorm_name VARCHAR, dormid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE has_amenity (dormid VARCHAR, amenid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE dorm_amenity (amenid VARCHAR, amenity_name VARCHAR)
original_sql: SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM dorm AS T1 JOIN has_amenity AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid JOIN dorm_amenity AS T3 ON T2.amenid = T3.amenid WHERE T3.amenity_name = 'TV Lounge' EXCEPT SELECT T1.dorm_name FROM dorm AS T1 JOIN has_amenity AS T2 ON T1.dormid = T2.dormid JOIN dorm_amenity AS T3 ON T2.amenid = T3.amenid WHERE T3.amenity_name = 'Study Room'
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    dorm_name,
    dormid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_amenity.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    dormid,
    amenid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'has_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm_amenity.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    amenid,
    amenity_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: dorm
      columns:
        - name: dormid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: has_amenity
      columns:
        - name: dormid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: dorm_amenity
      columns:
        - name: amenid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_dorm
    columns:
      - name: dorm_name
      - name: dormid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_has_amenity
    columns:
      - name: dormid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: amenid
  - name: stg_dorm_amenity
    columns:
      - name: amenid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: amenity_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_name_dorms_lounge.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH dorm AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_dorm') }}

),

has_amenity AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_has_amenity') }}

),

dorm_amenity AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_dorm_amenity') }}

)

SELECT dorm.dorm_name
FROM dorm
JOIN has_amenity ON dorm.dormid = dorm.dormid
JOIN dorm_amenity ON has_amenity.amenid = has_amenity.amenid
WHERE dorm_amenity.amenity_name = 'TV Lounge'
EXCEPT
SELECT dorm.dorm_name
FROM dorm
JOIN has_amenity ON dorm.dormid = dorm.dormid
JOIN dorm_amenity ON has_amenity.amenid = has_amenity.amenid
WHERE dorm_amenity.amenity_name = 'Study Room'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_name_dorms_lounge
    description: "Find the name of dorms which have TV Lounge but no Study Room as amenity.."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per distinct result in set operation"
    columns:
      - name: dorm_name
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

question: Show all artist names with an average exhibition attendance over 200.
context: CREATE TABLE artist (name VARCHAR, artist_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE exhibition (exhibition_id VARCHAR, artist_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE exhibition_record (exhibition_id VARCHAR, attendance INTEGER)
original_sql: SELECT T3.name FROM exhibition_record AS T1 JOIN exhibition AS T2 ON T1.exhibition_id = T2.exhibition_id JOIN artist AS T3 ON T3.artist_id = T2.artist_id GROUP BY T3.artist_id HAVING AVG(T1.attendance) > 200

{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    name,
    artist_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'artist') }}

SELECT
    exhibition_id,
    artist_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'exhibition') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_exhibition_record.sql">

    attendance

</file>

    tables:
      - name: artist
      columns:
        - name: artist_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: exhibition
      columns:
        - name: exhibition_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: exhibition_record
      columns:
        - name: exhibition_id
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_artist
    columns:
      - name: name
      - name: artist_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_exhibition
    columns:
      - name: exhibition_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: artist_id
  - name: stg_exhibition_record
    columns:
      - name: exhibition_id
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: attendance
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_exhibition_record_averages.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH exhibition_record AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_exhibition_record') }}

),

exhibition AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_exhibition') }}

),

artist AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_artist') }}

)

SELECT artist.name
FROM exhibition_record
JOIN exhibition ON exhibition.exhibition_id = exhibition.exhibition_id
JOIN artist ON artist.artist_id = artist.artist_id
GROUP BY artist.artist_id
HAVING AVG(exhibition_record.attendance) > 200
</file>

version: 2

models:
  - name: fct_exhibition_record_averages
    description: "Show all artist names with an average exhibition attendance over 200.."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per artist_id"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

original_sql: SELECT t1.fname FROM student AS t1 JOIN has_pet AS t2 ON t1.stuid = t2.stuid JOIN pets AS t3 ON t3.petid = t2.petid WHERE t3.pettype = 'cat' INTERSECT SELECT t1.fname FROM student AS t1 JOIN has_pet AS t2 ON t1.stuid = t2.stuid JOIN pets AS t3 ON t3.petid = t2.petid WHERE t3.pettype = 'dog'
dbt_dag:
<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    fname,
    stuid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_pet.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    stuid,
    petid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'has_pet') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_pets.sql">
{% config(materialized='view') %}

SELECT
    petid,
    pettype
FROM {{ source('raw', 'pets') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: student
      columns:
        - name: stuid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: has_pet
      columns:
        - name: stuid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique

      - name: pets
      columns:
        - name: petid
          tests:
            - not_null
            - unique
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: fname
      - name: stuid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
  - name: stg_has_pet
    columns:
      - name: stuid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: petid
  - name: stg_pets
    columns:
      - name: petid
        tests:
          - not_null
          - unique
      - name: pettype
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_first_name_students.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH student AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}

),

has_pet AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_has_pet') }}

),

pets AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_pets') }}

)

SELECT student.fname
FROM student
JOIN has_pet ON student.stuid = student.stuid
JOIN pets ON has_pet.petid = has_pet.petid
WHERE pets.pettype = 'cat'
INTERSECT
SELECT student.fname
FROM student
JOIN has_pet ON student.stuid = student.stuid
JOIN pets ON has_pet.petid = has_pet.petid
WHERE pets.pettype = 'dog'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

models:
  - name: rpt_first_name_students
    description: "Find the first name of students who have both cat and dog pets .."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per distinct result in set operation"
    columns:
      - name: fname
        tests:
          - not_null
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_first_names_offices.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH class AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_class') }}

),

employee AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }}

),

course AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }}

),

professor AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_professor') }}

),

department AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }}

)

SELECT employee.emp_fname, professor.prof_office, course.crs_description, department.dept_name
FROM class
JOIN employee ON class.prof_num = employee.emp_num
JOIN course ON course.crs_code = course.crs_code
JOIN professor ON employee.emp_num = employee.emp_num
JOIN department ON department.dept_code = department.dept_code
</file>

version: 2

models:

    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per class-employee pair"
    columns:
      - name: emp_fname
        tests:
          - not_null
      - name: prof_office
      - name: crs_description
      - name: dept_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_first_names_offices.sql">
{% config(materialized='table') %}

WITH class AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_class') }}

),

employee AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }}

),

course AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }}

),

professor AS (

    SELECT *
    FROM {{ ref('stg_professor') }}

)

SELECT employee.emp_fname, professor.prof_office, course.crs_description
FROM class
JOIN employee ON class.prof_num = professor.emp_num
JOIN course ON course.crs_code = course.crs_code
JOIN professor ON professor.emp_num = professor.emp_num
</file>
